description: WordPress Playground Blueprint reference for AI assistants. Use this to generate valid Blueprint JSON for start_playground.

landingPage.description: URL path to navigate to after the blueprint runs

php.description: PHP version to use. Default: latest

networking.description: Enable fetch()-based networking for wp_safe_remote_get, plugin/theme downloads from wordpress.org, etc. Default: true when using MCP (auto-injected)

extraLibraries.description: Extra libraries to load. Include 'wp-cli' to enable WP-CLI. Auto-included when any wp-cli steps exist or when using MCP (auto-injected)

plugins.description: Shorthand: array of plugin slugs from wordpress.org to install and activate. Example: ["woocommerce", "jetpack"]

siteOptions.description: Shorthand for setSiteOptions step. Key-value pairs of WordPress options. Example: { "blogname": "My Test Site" }

login.description: true to auto-login as admin (admin/password), or { "username": "...", "password": "..." }. Auto-injected as true by MCP

constants.description: PHP constants to define on every request. Example: { "WP_DEBUG": true, "WP_DEBUG_LOG": true }

steps.description: Sequential steps to execute after WordPress boots. Each step is an object with a 'step' property identifying the step type

activatePlugin.description: Activate an already-installed plugin by its directory/file path

params.pluginPath: string — Plugin path relative to wp-content/plugins/, e.g. 'my-plugin/my-plugin.php' or just 'my-plugin' for single-file plugins

cp.description: Copy a file within the virtual filesystem

params.consts: object — Key-value pairs of constants. Values can be string, number, or boolean

enableMultisite.description: Enable WordPress multisite. Must run before most other steps

importWxr.description: Import WordPress WXR (export) file to populate content

params.file: FileResource — A resource reference (url, literal, vfs) pointing to a .wxr or .xml file

importThemeStarterContent.description: Import the active theme's starter content

params.themeSlug: string — Theme slug. If omitted, uses the active theme

importWordPressFiles.description: Import WordPress files from a zip archive

params.wordPressFilesZip: FileResource — A resource reference pointing to a zip of WordPress files

installPlugin.description: Install a plugin from wordpress.org, a URL, inline code, or a directory

params.pluginData: FileRef or DirectoryRef — The plugin source. Use { resource: 'wordpress.org/plugins', slug: 'plugin-slug' } for wordpress.org plugins
params.options: object — { activate: boolean } — Whether to activate after install. Default: true

installTheme.description: Install a theme from wordpress.org, a URL, or other source

params.themeData: FileRef or DirectoryRef — The theme source. Use { resource: 'wordpress.org/themes', slug: 'theme-slug' } for wordpress.org themes

login.description: Log in as a WordPress user

params.username: string — Username to log in as. Default: 'admin'

mkdir.description: Create a directory in the virtual filesystem

mv.description: Move/rename a file in the virtual filesystem

resetData.description: Reset the WordPress database to a fresh state

request.description: Make an HTTP request to the WordPress instance

params.request: PHPRequest — { url: string, method?: string, headers?: object, body?: string }

rm.description: Remove a file from the virtual filesystem

params.path: string — Path to the file to remove

rmdir.description: Remove a directory from the virtual filesystem

params.path: string — Path to the directory to remove

runPHP.description: Run arbitrary PHP code. The code MUST start with <?php. To use WordPress functions, include: require_once '/wordpress/wp-load.php';

params.code: string — PHP code to execute. Must start with '<?php'

runPHPWithOptions.description: Run PHP with additional options like POST body or custom headers

params.options: object — { code: string, method?: string, body?: string, headers?: object, relativeUrl?: string }

params.sql: ResourceType — A resource containing the SQL to execute

params.options: object — Key-value pairs of option names and values

unzip.description: Extract a zip archive in the virtual filesystem

params.zipPath: string — Path to the zip file

wp-cli.description: Run a WP-CLI command. The command string should include the 'wp' prefix. Requires extraLibraries: ['wp-cli'] (auto-included by MCP)

params.command: string — The full WP-CLI command, e.g. 'wp post list --format=json'
params.wpCliPath: string — Optional custom path to wp-cli.phar

writeFile.description: Write content to a file in the virtual filesystem. Creates parent directories as needed

params.path: string — Absolute path for the file
params.data: string or FileResource — File contents as a string or a resource reference

example.data:
<?php
/**
 * Plugin Name: My Plugin
 * Description: A test plugin
 * Version: 1.0.0
 */

add_action('init', function() {
    // Plugin code here
});

writeFiles.description: Write multiple files at once using a directory tree structure

params.filesTree: DirectoryResource — A directory reference, typically literal:directory with inline file contents

wordpress.org/plugins.description: Install a plugin from the WordPress.org plugin directory

wordpress.org/themes.description: Install a theme from the WordPress.org theme directory

url.description: Download a file from any URL

properties.url: string — Full URL to download from
properties.caption: string (optional) — Display caption for progress

literal.description: Inline file contents as a string

vfs.description: Reference a file already in the Playground virtual filesystem

properties.path: string — Absolute path in the VFS

git:directory.description: Clone a directory from a Git repository

properties.ref: string — Branch, tag, or commit hash

literal:directory.description: Inline a directory structure with file contents

properties.name: string — Base directory path
properties.files: object — Key-value pairs where keys are relative file paths and values are file contents (string)

zip.description: Wrap a resource as a ZIP archive

properties.inner: FileRef or DirectoryRef — The resource to zip

examples.title: Basic WordPress with a plugin from wordpress.org
examples.description: Install and activate WooCommerce on a fresh WordPress site

examples.title: WooCommerce setup with sample products via WP-CLI
examples.description: Boot WooCommerce and create sample products using WP-CLI steps

examples.title: Custom plugin from inline code
examples.description: Write a custom plugin directly using writeFile and activate it

steps.data:
Hello from my custom plugin!

'; }); add_shortcode('hello', function($atts) { $name = $atts['name'] ?? 'World'; return '

Hello, ' . esc_html($name) . '!

'; });

examples.title: Full setup with theme, plugins, site options, and content
examples.description: Complete site setup with Twenty Twenty-Four theme, plugins, custom options, and WXR content import

steps.command: wp post create --post_title='Welcome to My Site' --post_content='This is a test site running on WordPress Playground.' --post_status=publish
